OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 56-year-old waiter from Rome, I've never been too fond of social media. I have no accounts and I don't miss it. But I know how it can be a breeding ground for fake content. My parents taught me to be critical of what I read and hear, especially when it comes to politics and news. My mother, who has a PhD, always told me to fact-check and verify sources before accepting any information as true. My father, who never had formal education, would often say that 'if it sounds too good to be true, it probably is.' I grew up with these values and I find it hard to understand why people can't be more discerning. I mean, if you're getting your news from a random Facebook post, shouldn't you be questioning its credibility? It's not that hard to be critical, but I guess it requires some effort and awareness. Anyway, my experience with fake content is limited to what I see and hear from others, but I try to stay informed and educated, and I think that's key to navigating the online world. I wish more people would be as cautious as I am, but I guess that's just not how it works. I do yoga and read novels, and those hobbies help me relax and focus on what's real. I don't have much time for social media, but I do think it's essential to manage and deal with fake content on these platforms. It's not about getting rid of it altogether, but about being aware of it and knowing how to spot it. It's a skill that takes practice, but it's worth it in the end. I'm not a tech expert, but I think social media companies should do more to combat fake content. It's their responsibility, after all. Anyway, that's my two cents on the matter. It's not a solution, but it's a start.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I based my opinion on my parents' values and education level, as well as my own critical thinking and awareness. I also drew from my experience as a waiter and my hobbies, which help me stay grounded and focused. I tried to be as objective as possible, but I guess that's not always easy. I wish I could do more to help, but I think I can at least share my perspective and insights.
citations: a news article about social media and fake content
